get.description:
> 🚧 This endpoint is in beta and will be released in GA in our 2026-10-15 revision. > > A beta revision header (2026-07-15.pre) is required to use our beta APIs. Get all campaigns, with optional filtering.

*Rate limits*:
Burst: `10/s`
Steady: `150/m` **Scopes:** `campaigns:read` [OpenAPI Spec](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/klaviyo/openapi/main/openapi/beta/apis/get_campaigns_beta.json)

parameters.name: filter
parameters.description:
For more information please visit https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/v2026-07-15.pre/reference/api-overview#filtering

`id`: `any`
`name`: `contains`

present.

Per https://jsonapi.org/format/#crud-updating-resource-attributes, only
the top-level attributes/relationships present in the request body are
replaced; missing top-level fields are preserved. A client can patch
just `name` without disturbing `width`, `alt_text`, the `image`
relationship, etc.

A present top-level field is replaced wholesale (no recursive merge
into nested structures), so sending `"alt_text": null` clears it.

The `image` relationship is optional on PATCH — omit to keep the
existing image; include with a non-null `data` to repoint at a
different image. A logo *must* have an image, so the JSON:API
"unlink" form (`"image": {"data": null}`) is not supported and is
treated the same as omitting the relationship (existing image
preserved).

requestBody.description:
PATCH /api/brand-voice/{company_id} — JSON:API field-level partial update.

Per https://jsonapi.org/format/#crud-updating-resource-attributes, only
the top-level attributes present in the request body are replaced;
missing top-level fields are preserved. A client can patch just
`tone_descriptors` without disturbing `language_patterns`, and vice
versa.

A present top-level field is replaced wholesale (no recursive merge
into the list contents) — the underlying service treats
`tone_descriptors` and `language_patterns` as full-list replacements,
so partial-list edits must be done client-side by reading, modifying,
and writing the full list.

The path `id` must equal the auth context's company id; mismatches
return 404.

color.description: Campaign display color as a hex string (e.g. 'ff5733')

description.description: A short description of the campaign

send_time.description: The UTC send time for this message

send_times.description: Per-variation send times, keyed by variation ID. Populated for send-time optimization and AB send time test strategies.

send_strategy.description: The scheduling strategy for this message.

recipient_percentage.description: The percentage of recipients to send this message to

throttling_type.description: The throttling type for this message

throttle_unit.description: The time unit each batch of recipients is sent over for throttled sends.

is_ignoring_unsubscribes.description: Whether to ignore unsubscribes for this message

tracking_options.description: The tracking options for this message

send_options.description: The send options for this message

created.description: The datetime when this message was created

updated.description: The datetime when this message was updated

id.description: The content variations for this message

included.description: The list of included group IDs

excluded.description: The list of excluded group IDs

created.description: The datetime when this audience was created

updated.description: The datetime when this audience was last updated

created_at.description: The datetime when the campaign was created

updated_at.description: The datetime when the campaign was last updated

id.description: The audiences associated with the campaign
The messages associated with the campaign
The tags associated with the campaign

text_message_type.description:
A sms message variation's text message type.

Values match the Django OmniSMSTextMessageType (MESSAGE_FORMAT_*)
    constants.

action_type.description: The action type for a push notification action button.

badge_config.description: A push message variation's badge config.

push_type.description: A push message variation's push type.

details.description: The channel-specific content details for this variation

created.description: The datetime when this variation was created

updated.description: The datetime when this variation was updated

id.description: The associated static image for mobile push variations

type.description: The DNS record type (cname, txt, mx).

host.description: The DNS hostname that must be configured by the customer.

value.description: The value the DNS record must resolve to.

verified.description: Whether Klaviyo has verified that this DNS record resolves correctly.

id.description: The ID of the sending domain. Generated by Klaviyo.

dkim_selector.description: The DKIM selector used to locate the public key in DNS.

purpose.description: What this sending domain is used for.

configuration.description: How the customer publishes DNS for this domain. `static` exposes per-ESP CNAME/TXT records the customer publishes directly. `dynamic` exposes the 4 NS delegation records pointing at `ns{1..4}.klaviyo.com`; Klaviyo then manages the per-ESP records inside the delegated zone.

status.description: The lifecycle status of this sending domain for the calling account. Run a verification job (`POST /api/sending-domain-verification-jobs`) followed by an activation job (`POST /api/sending-domain-activation-jobs`) to move a domain from `pending` to `warming` or `active`.

dns_records.description: DNS records the customer must add to their domain for Klaviyo to validate and send on their behalf. For `static` configuration, includes CNAMEs for the mail host and DKIM selectors and, when applicable, a TXT record for site verification. For `dynamic` configuration, exposes the 4 NS delegation records — the per-ESP CNAMEs inside the delegated zone are managed by Klaviyo and are not surfaced here.

created_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the sending domain record was created.

updated_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the sending domain record was last updated.

id.description: The unique identifier for this text messaging configuration.

org_prefix.description: Branding prefix prepended to outbound messages for sender identification. Up to 50 characters.

opt_out_language.description: Opt-out instruction text appended to outbound messages.

status.description: The lifecycle status of this text messaging configuration.

created_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the configuration was created.

updated_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the configuration was last updated.

country.description: ISO 3166-1 alpha-2 country code the sender is provisioned in.

id.description: The unique identifier for this sender registration.

status.description: The lifecycle status of this sender registration.

created_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the registration was created.

updated_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the registration was last updated.

submitted_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the registration was submitted. Null until the customer submits the registration.

decided_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the registration was approved or rejected. Null until the carrier returns a decision.

id.description: The unique identifier for this text-messaging sender.

sender_type.description: The kind of sender (e.g. toll-free, short-code, long-code, alpha).

sender_details.description: Attributes specific to the sender's type.

status.description: The lifecycle status of this sender.

created_at.description: ISO8601 timestamp when the sender was created.

forwarding_number.description: E.164 phone number inbound voice calls are forwarded to. Null when no forwarding is configured.

sender_identifier.description: The sender's outbound address — E.164 phone number for phone-number senders. Null while the sender is still pending registration.

id.description: The unique identifier for this sender registration.

name.description: Human-readable label set on the key.

status.description: Whether the key is active or disabled.

Private key authentication for /api/ endpoints is performed by setting the `Authorization` header to `Klaviyo-API-Key your-private-api-key`
For more information please visit https://developers.klaviyo.com/en/v2026-07-15.pre/reference/api-overview#authentication
